prd_meta.title: Analyze v2 — Brave-Backed Retrieval Migration PRD
prd_meta.description: Build a parallel POST /api/v2/companies/{ticker}/analyze endpoint that replaces OpenRouter :online with the market-recap pattern (Brave Search + goggle + market-keyed allowlist + ranking + LLM stuff with inline source_id citations). Eval at tmp/analyze_eval/full01 justifies migration: brave wins 12/15, faster, real URLs vs 100% opaque vertex redirects. v1 stays mounted; FE cuts over to v2.

related_documents.usage_note: If implementation details are ambiguous, consult the design plan first, then update this PRD with phase learnings.

scope.frontend: cuts over fully to v2 once FE renderer ships
scope.persistence: no DB; structured JSON logs keyed by analyze_request_id

prd_meta.sorting_rule: phases ordered by technical dependency chain; each phase independently mergeable

core_non_negotiables.tdd_required: Every phase MUST follow /tdd: write failing tests first, then implementation. Tests committed before or with implementation in the same PR. No implementation-only commits.
core_non_negotiables.no_external_api_in_tests: Tests must NEVER hit Brave or OpenRouter live. Use committed fixtures under tests/services/analyze_retrieval/fixtures/. tmp/ is gitignored and MUST NOT be referenced from tests.
core_non_negotiables.grounding: v2 LLM consumes only Brave-retrieved passages stuffed into the prompt. No :online mode in v2.
core_non_negotiables.citation_contract: LLM emits [N] inline. Backend streams raw text WITH [N] markers preserved (no buffering, no stripping). After stream ends, emit a single sources event mapping each N -> {source_id, url, title, publisher, published_at, is_trusted}. No paragraph_done event. No [SOURCES_JSON] block. FE renders [N] -> badge using the final sources event.
core_non_negotiables.fail_fast: On Brave error or 0 results, emit {type: error, code: retrieval_failed} and close stream. No silent :online fallback.
core_non_negotiables.no_db_persistence: No new table. Debug via structured JSON logs keyed on analyze_request_id (UUID4) emitted as first SSE meta event.
core_non_negotiables.v1_v2_cache_isolation: SemanticAnalysisCache key prefix bumped to v2: to prevent cross-version replay.
core_non_negotiables.v2_cache_ttl: v2 SemanticAnalysisCache entries expire after 30 minutes regardless of category. Bounds staleness for time-sensitive answers backed by Brave retrieval.
core_non_negotiables.search_decision_respected: v2 honors the existing SearchDecisionEngine. When the engine says 'no search', v2 takes a non-grounded path (no Brave call, no sources event). meta event with request_id is ALWAYS emitted, regardless of search decision.
core_non_negotiables.verbatim_query: Pass user prompt verbatim to Brave; no rewriter.
core_non_negotiables.language_handling: No explicit language directive. Trust Gemini30Flash multilingual. Citations remain in source language.
core_non_negotiables.rate_limiting: No proactive throttling. Monitor 429s in prod; add reactively if observed.

agent_validation_protocol.purpose: Allow implementation agent to prove phase success without human hand-holding.

phase_transition_policy.rule: Agent stops after each phase and waits for explicit user approval before starting the next phase, even when all gates pass.

tdd_enforcement_policy.rule: Each phase MUST start by writing failing tests that encode the phase's definition_of_done. Implementation only proceeds once tests are red. Phase cannot be marked complete unless those same tests are green AND committed.
evidence_required: Test file paths committed alongside implementation

learning_feedback_policy.rule: After each phase, agent writes validation_summary, learnings, gates_result_snapshot, and next_phase_considerations into this PRD under that phase.
learning_feedback_policy.next_phase_requirement: Before starting a phase, agent reviews and incorporates previous phase learnings.

requirements_for_every_phase: Run phase tests + project baseline checks (healthcheck + ruff).
Collect evidence: red test log, green test log, command outputs, SSE samples where relevant.
Update this PRD with validation_summary, learnings, gates_result_snapshot before requesting approval.

baseline_check_policy.rule: tests/test_healthcheck.py and ruff check . are mandatory hard gates for every phase.

phases.title: Chat-specific source policy (allowlist + tiering + discards)
phases.objective: Define and codify the trusted-source policy used by the v2 goggle. Two buckets only: VN (Vietnamese tickers) and GLOBAL (US/FI/everything else). Two boost tiers. Recap's allowlist is NOT shared — analyze v2 owns its own policy file.
scope_includes: New module services/analyze_retrieval/source_policy.py independent of services/market_recap/source_policy.py
Two markets: GLOBAL (default) and VN
Two tiers per market: TIER_1 (boost=4) and TIER_2 (boost=2)
Optional FI extension list appended to GLOBAL when market=FI (Finnish-language sources)
Hard discards list (social media + opinion blog platforms)
is_trusted(url, market) helper that returns True for any tier
tier_for(url, market) helper returning 1 | 2 | None
scope_excludes: No goggle building yet (phase-1 consumes this policy)
No changes to services/market_recap/source_policy.py — recap handled separately
No tier-3 / no scoring weight tuning (deferred to phase-8 if eval shows need)
phases.extra_setup_step: Copy 5 representative brave.json files (one per category: csf, cg, news, etf, vn) from tmp/analyze_eval/full01/<id>/brave.json into tests/services/analyze_retrieval/fixtures/ and commit. tmp/ is gitignored — fixtures MUST live under tests/.

red_tests: tier_for('https://reuters.com/article/x', 'GLOBAL') == 1
tier_for('https://investing.com/x', 'GLOBAL') == 2
tier_for('https://reddit.com/r/x', 'GLOBAL') is None
tier_for('https://inderes.fi/x', 'FI') == 1 (FI extension applied on top of GLOBAL)
tier_for('https://reuters.com/x', 'FI') == 1 (GLOBAL still applies for FI)
tier_for('https://cafef.vn/x', 'VN') == 1
tier_for('https://reuters.com/x', 'VN') is None (VN does NOT inherit GLOBAL)
is_trusted is True for any tier, False for None and discards
is_discarded('https://medium.com/x') is True
Subdomain match: tier_for('https://www.reuters.com/x', 'GLOBAL') == 1

implementation_checks: Module is independent of services/market_recap/source_policy.py — no imports across
Domain matching uses registrable_domain (handles www. and other subdomains)
Wildcard *.gov handled correctly (matches any .gov subdomain)
Discards take precedence over allowlists (defense-in-depth)
Markets are an Enum or Literal, not free-form strings

required_evidence: Initial failing test run (red)
Final passing test run (green)
Snapshot of resolved tier-1/tier-2 lists per market printed during a test
phase_gates: Gate B: GLOBAL/VN isolation verified (VN does not inherit GLOBAL)
Gate C: FI extension stacks on GLOBAL correctly

phases.title: Retrieval primitives (market, goggle, publisher, schemas)
phases.objective: Establish pure-function building blocks the orchestrator depends on. No I/O.
scope_includes: services/analyze_retrieval/ skeleton + __init__.py
market.py: resolve_market(country, question_text) -> 'US'|'VN'|'FI'

goggle.py: build_chat_goggle(market) -> str. Reads tier-1/tier-2 lists from services/analyze_retrieval/source_policy and emits $boost=4,site=<tier1> + $boost=2,site=<tier2> + $discard=<discards> lines
publisher.py: publisher_label_for(url) -> str (lookup table + registrable_domain fallback)
schemas.py: AnalyzeSource, AnalyzeRetrievalResult, BraveRetrievalError
scope_excludes: No ranking, no orchestrator, no handlers

red_tests: test_market: USA/Vietnam/Finland country -> US/VN/FI; unknown -> US; Vietnamese diacritics in question with no country -> VN
test_goggle: contains $boost=4,site=<tier1> and $boost=2,site=<tier2> lines per market; tier-1 entries do NOT also appear at boost=2
test_goggle: discards from policy emitted as $discard=<domain> lines (social + opinion-blog domains)
test_goggle: market=FI emits GLOBAL tier-1/2 + FI extension tier-1/2
test_goggle: market=VN emits ONLY VN lines (no GLOBAL inheritance)
test_publisher: known domains map to human label (reuters.com -> Reuters); unknown -> titlecased registrable domain
test_schemas: AnalyzeSource validates required fields; BraveRetrievalError is an Exception subclass

implementation_checks: All four modules import cleanly with no circular deps
Goggle output parity-checked against recap goggle for shared markets
Vietnamese language detection heuristic deterministic on fixture strings

required_evidence: Initial failing test run (red)
Final passing test run (green)
Goggle string sample for each market
phase_gates: Gate B: all phase tests pass
Gate C: healthcheck + ruff baseline pass

phases.title: Brave client wrapper + ranking
phases.objective: Wrap Brave search for chat with no-freshness param, plus deterministic ranking over candidates. No live API in tests.
scope_includes: brave_client.py: thin wrapper around recap BraveClient OR new method search_no_freshness(); decision committed in code

ranking.py: rank_for_chat(candidates, market) -> list[Candidate]. Order: tier-1 first, then tier-2, then untrusted; within tier prefer recent (published_at <90 days) over older, then content length desc, then brave score desc. Tier comes from services/analyze_retrieval/source_policy.tier_for(...). Recency is a soft boost only — no hard cutoff.
Integration test using fixtures from tmp/analyze_eval/full01/<id>/brave.json
scope_excludes: No live Brave calls in tests

red_tests: test_ranking: tier-1 ranks above tier-2 ranks above untrusted; within tier, recent (<90d) beats older; then longer content beats shorter; ties broken by brave score desc then URL
test_ranking: missing published_at treated as 'old' (no recency boost) — does not crash
test_ranking: empty input -> empty output
test_brave_client: parses canned Brave fixture into Candidate list with canonical URLs
test_brave_client: HTTP error -> raises BraveRetrievalError

implementation_checks: brave_client passes freshness=None (or omits it) per Q4 decision
Ranking is stable for ties (deterministic tiebreaker on URL)
No live API calls during pytest run

required_evidence: Red then green test logs
Fixture file paths committed
Confirmation that no network egress occurred during tests (e.g. requests-mock or responses lib used)
phase_gates: Gate B: zero live API calls in tests

phases.title: Retrieval orchestrator + structured logging
phases.objective: Wire query -> brave -> dedupe -> rank -> top_k into a single retrieve_for_analyze function. Emit one JSON log line per request keyed on analyze_request_id.
scope_includes: retrieval.py: retrieve_for_analyze(question, market, top_k=5) -> AnalyzeRetrievalResult; raises BraveRetrievalError on empty
observability.py: log_retrieval(request_id, ticker, market, ...) -> emits one JSON line via app logger
Dedup by canonical_url; drop empty raw_content
scope_excludes: No DB writes — Q8 locked: structured logs only

red_tests: test_retrieval: end-to-end against canned brave fixture -> returns AnalyzeRetrievalResult with top_k=5 sources, all source_ids unique
test_retrieval: empty result -> raises BraveRetrievalError
test_retrieval: duplicate canonical URLs are deduped
test_observability: emits exactly one JSON log line containing required fields (request_id, ticker, market, ranked_urls, selected_source_ids, brave_latency_ms)
test_observability: raw Brave response is NOT in the log payload

implementation_checks: Orchestrator wires phase-1 + phase-2 modules with no leakage of HTTP types upward
Log line is single JSON, parseable, no PII beyond question text already in conversation

required_evidence: Red then green test logs
Sample captured log line (redacted if needed)
phase_gates: Gate B: orchestrator + observability tests pass
Gate C: no DB schema change introduced

phases.title: Citation index collector (final sources event builder)
phases.objective: Tiny utility that scans the streamed model text for [N] occurrences and produces the final sources event payload mapping N -> source metadata. Does NOT buffer the stream; does NOT strip [N] markers.
scope_includes: build_sources_event(full_text: str, retrieved_sources: list[AnalyzeSource]) -> dict — extracts unique [N] tokens, maps each to retrieved_sources[N-1], returns {type: 'sources', body: {sources: [...]}}
Detect and log out-of-range [N] (e.g. [9] when only 5 sources retrieved) — exclude from output, increment a counter for observability
Detect unused sources (retrieved but never cited by LLM) — included in final sources list anyway? No — only emit cited sources. Log uncited count.
scope_excludes: No stream buffering — text streams raw to client token-by-token
No paragraph_done event — dropped from schema

red_tests: Text containing [1] and [3] only -> sources event lists s_1 and s_3, omits s_2/s_4/s_5
Text containing [1][2][1] -> sources event has s_1 once (deduped) and s_2
Text containing [9] when 5 sources retrieved -> dropped from output, warning logged
Text containing no [N] -> sources event with empty sources list
Each emitted source has full metadata (source_id, url, title, publisher, published_at, is_trusted)

implementation_checks: Pure async generator; no I/O beyond logging
Deterministic output across chunk boundaries

required_evidence: Red then green test logs
Sample fixtures showing chunking edge cases
phase_gates: Gate B: chunking correctness across boundaries

phases.title: CompanyGeneralHandlerV2 (smallest handler first)
phases.objective: Port simplest handler to v2 pattern: retrieval -> stuff prompt -> stream -> paragraph events. Establishes the shape used by remaining handlers.
scope_includes: CompanyGeneralHandlerV2: takes retrieval result, builds [N]-stuffed prompt, streams Gemini30Flash, yields raw answer chunks (with [N] preserved), then a final sources event built via citation_index.build_sources_event
Single combined thinking_status event before generation: 'Reading 5 sources: Reuters, CNBC, FT, Bloomberg, SEC' — one event listing all trusted publishers, no per-source sequential events
If retrieval was skipped (SearchDecisionEngine said no), handler runs without sources/thinking_status — just answer stream, no final sources event
scope_excludes: Other handlers (specific finance, comparison, ETF) — separate phases

red_tests: Given canned passages + mocked LLM stream emitting '[1][2]', handler yields: single thinking_status with all publishers in body -> raw answer chunks containing [1][2] verbatim -> final sources event with s_1, s_2 metadata only
Combined thinking_status body lists only trusted (tier-1/tier-2) publishers; untrusted are omitted
LLM stream with no [N] -> final sources event has empty sources list
Search-decision = no_search path -> handler emits answer only, no thinking_status, no sources event
No live LLM call in tests (mocked OpenRouterClient)

implementation_checks: Handler returns AsyncIterator of dict events compatible with v1 SSE serializer
Sources event is emitted exactly once after stream end

required_evidence: Red then green test logs
Sample event sequence captured from a unit test
phase_gates: Gate B: event sequence matches PRD schema
Gate C: zero live LLM/Brave calls in tests

phases.title: Remaining handlers v2 (general finance, specific finance, comparison, ETF)
phases.objective: Port remaining handlers to v2 pattern using shape established in phase 5. Per-handler PRs recommended.
scope_includes: GeneralFinanceHandlerV2
CompanySpecificFinanceHandlerV2 (DB financials + Brave passages; trim raw_content if context blows up)

CompanyComparisonHandlerV2: per-ticker Brave query fanned out via asyncio.gather with concurrency cap = 5; on partial failure proceed with successes + add a note to the answer naming the failed ticker(s); only fail the whole request if ALL ticker retrievals fail
ETFAnalyzerV2 + ETF handlers v2

red_tests: Per-handler: canned passages + mocked LLM -> expected SSE event sequence
CompanySpecificFinanceHandlerV2: when DB context large, raw_content trimmed to 3000 chars per passage
ComparisonHandlerV2: N tickers -> N parallel retrieval calls via asyncio.gather (mocked); total elapsed in test approximates max(per-call latency), not sum
ComparisonHandlerV2: 1 of 4 tickers fails retrieval -> handler still produces an answer using the 3 successful tickers' sources, and the prompt context includes a marker noting the failed ticker
ComparisonHandlerV2: ALL tickers fail retrieval -> raises BraveRetrievalError
ETF: existing context_builders preserved alongside Brave

implementation_checks: All v2 handlers emit consistent event schema
FinancialAnalyzerV2.analyze_question dispatches by classifier output (reused)
v1 handlers untouched

required_commands: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/services/question_analyzer/ tests/services/test_etf_analyzer_v2.py -v
required_evidence: Red then green logs per handler
v1 test suite still green (regression check)
phase_gates: Gate A: TDD red->green proof for each handler
Gate B: v1 regression suite green
Gate C: zero live external calls in tests

phases.title: POST /api/v2/companies/{ticker}/analyze
phases.objective: Mount the v2 endpoint parallel to v1, with request_id meta event, error stream contract, and v2: cache namespace.
scope_includes: main.py: new route /api/v2/companies/{ticker}/analyze
Mint analyze_request_id (UUID4); emit as first SSE event {type:meta, body:{request_id}}
Bump SemanticAnalysisCache key prefix to v2: AND set entry TTL to 30 minutes for v2 entries (uniform across categories)
v2 endpoint always honors SearchDecisionEngine — when it returns no_search, skip retrieval and run handler in non-grounded mode (still emits meta event with request_id)
BraveRetrievalError -> {type:error, code:retrieval_failed} + clean stream close
Startup healthcheck: BRAVE_API_KEY present

red_tests: POST /api/v2/companies/AAPL/analyze with mocked retrieval+LLM -> first SSE event is meta with valid UUID request_id
Brave error path -> stream contains {type:error, code:retrieval_failed} then closes
Cache: v1 entry does not satisfy v2 request and vice versa
Cache: v2 entry written at t=0 is NOT returned at t=31min (TTL expired); IS returned at t=29min
SearchDecisionEngine returns no_search -> endpoint skips retrieval; meta event still emitted; no sources event; answer streams normally
Missing BRAVE_API_KEY at startup -> healthcheck fails (test via env override)

implementation_checks: v1 endpoint untouched and still works
v2 endpoint uses identical conversation/cookie flow as v1
Cache namespace isolation verified

required_evidence: Red then green test logs
Sample SSE transcript from a test client (request_id meta first)
Confirmation that v1 tests still pass
phase_gates: Gate B: v1 regression suite green
Gate C: SSE schema matches PRD (meta first, error contract honored)

phases.title: End-to-end validation against eval harness
phases.objective: Prove v2 endpoint reproduces (or beats) the brave arm of the existing eval; capture latency vs v1.
scope_includes: Extend scripts/eval_analyze_search/run_eval.py with --arm v2-endpoint that POSTs the v2 endpoint
Run against the 15-prompt set; compare to captured brave arm
Manual smoke: 5 prompts via curl, inspect SSE stream
Capture v2 p50/p95 vs v1

red_tests: Unit test for the new --arm v2-endpoint dispatcher (mocked HTTP)
Smoke assertion: each captured response has at least 1 source_id and request_id meta event

implementation_checks: v2 endpoint answers parity (or better) on >=11/15 prompts vs brave arm (judged or manual)
p50 latency within +20% of brave arm in eval
All 15 prompts return without retrieval_failed under happy path

required_commands: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/eval_analyze_search/run_eval.py --arm v2-endpoint tmp/analyze_eval/v2_endpoint_run
source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/eval_analyze_search/aggregate.py tmp/analyze_eval/v2_endpoint_run
required_evidence: Aggregate stats output captured in PRD validation_summary
5 manual SSE transcripts attached as evidence
p50/p95 latency table v1 vs v2
phase_gates: Gate C: no retrieval_failed under happy path

phases.title: Frontend handoff doc
phases.objective: Hand frontend the v2 SSE contract so they can ship the inline-citation renderer and switch the endpoint URL.
scope_includes: Document v2 SSE event schema in a single markdown delivered to FE
Include a sample SSE transcript per category
Note the request_id meta event for debug/reporting flows

red_tests: Doc-driven test: a fixture transcript embedded in the doc is parsed by a small unit test to ensure it matches the live SSE schema (drift detector)

implementation_checks: Doc covers every event type with example payload
Drift detector passes
FE confirms ack (out-of-band)

required_evidence: Drift test green
Doc path linked in PR

global_unresolved_questions: Q6.1: Strip [N] markers backend-side or pass through? Default: strip.
Q14.1: Comparison handler — per-ticker queries (default) or single combined?
Q6.3: When DB context + Brave passages are large, trim raw_content to 3000 chars/passage — confirm threshold once measured.
Q3.1: Country normalization table — what other values appear in company_fundamental.data['country']? Run a quick distinct query before phase 1.
Q6.4: Retain :online as hidden ops flag in v2? Default: no.
